# zod-crud

> All frontend editing is JSON editing. zod-crud provides Zod-guarded headless
> JSON editing primitives. Root `zod-crud` is React-free;
> React hooks live under `zod-crud/react`. State, operations, selection,
> history, and wire payloads are serializable JSON.

## Identity

zod-crud is a reusable FE editing vocabulary layer for JSON documents:
Selection / Edit / Clipboard / Undo are mapped to JSON standards
(RFC 6901 Pointer, RFC 6902 Patch, RFC 9535 JSONPath, RFC 8259 JSON,
W3C Selection vocabulary, JSON Schema/Zod). It is not a UI component library
and should not be described as an editor.

## Canonical Entry Points

```ts
// Headless document, selection, and clipboard facades.
import { createClipboard, createJSONDocument, createSelection, moveSelectionCursor, extendSelectionCursor, resolveSelectionCursor, selectSelectionScope, resolveSelectionScope, orderSelectionRange, orderPrimarySelectionRange, orderSelectionRanges, selectionSpansForPointer, selectionTextEdits, replaceSelectionText, deleteSelectionText } from "zod-crud";

// React facade and React-only helpers.
import {
  useJSONDocument,
} from "zod-crud/react";

// Pure core.
import { applyPatch, applyOperation, computeInverses } from "zod-crud";

// Pointer / serialization.
import { parsePointer, buildPointer, serialize, parse, safeParse } from "zod-crud";

```

Only import from package exports: `zod-crud` or `zod-crud/react`. Do not
import `zod-crud/src/*`, `zod-crud/dist/*`, or source-layout subpaths.

## Facade Surface

`createJSONDocument(schema, initial, options?)` and
`useJSONDocument(schema, initial, options?)` intentionally expose the same
document surface:

| Field | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `doc.value` | current schema-valid JSON value |
| `doc.lastPatch` | value snapshot of the last applied document patch; `[]` after selection-only commits |
| `doc.ops` | JSON Patch-like low-level operations plus load/reset/subscribe and facade undo/redo |
| `doc.commands` | edit command namespace; `remove(source?)` commits structural JSON deletion without clipboard payload, `replaceText(replacement, options?)` and `deleteText(options?)` commit selection text edits, `moveCursor(direction, options?)` / `extendCursor(direction, options?)` expose keyboard-style cursor movement, and `selectScope(options?)` exposes Ctrl+A/select-visible selection through the same selection mode |
| `doc.can` | command, JSONPath find, and selection guard namespace |
| `doc.check` | explainable dry-run guard namespace for commands, JSONPath find, selection movement, and selection text edits; `can.x(...) === check.x(...).ok` |
| `doc.schema` | serializable schema introspection; `at`/`kind`/`accepts`/`describe` |
| `doc.commit` | apply an RFC 6902 patch plus optional final `SelectionAction | SelectionSnap` as one history entry |
| `doc.at` / `doc.exists` | pointer read helpers |
| `doc.query` / `doc.entries` | JSONPath-to-pointer query and state-based child entries |
| `doc.history` | `canUndo`, `canRedo`, depths, `mergeLast(options?)`, `transaction(options?, fn)` |
| `doc.selection` | `JSONPoint`-based W3C-shaped selection when enabled; `selectionRanges` is the caret/range source of truth, `context` is optional JSON-serializable selection-local editing state for stored marks/active tools/find mode, `initial`/`selectRanges` accept `JSONPoint` or `{ anchor, focus }`, string caret offsets are clamped to current text, getters, `snapshot()`, and `toJSON()` return value-safe coordinates, `JSON.stringify(doc.selection)` emits that snapshot, `restore(snapshot)` restores it, `subscribe(listener)` emits JSON-safe snapshot transitions, `moveCursor`/`extendCursor` mutate cursor/range by JSON source-order within an optional scope, by RFC 9535 `query` results, or by explicit app-visible `points`, `resolveCursor` computes the same target without mutation, `selectScope` builds Ctrl+A/select-visible/select-find-results selections from that same traversal model, `orderPrimaryRange` converts directional anchor/focus into document-order start/end, `orderRanges` does the same for every range while preserving original range indexes and primary flag, `spansForPointer(pointer)` clips selection into pointer-local spans for rendering/offset edits, `textEdits(replacement)` plans ordered pointer-local text replacements, `textPatch(replacement)` builds string-leaf RFC 6902 replace patches plus final collapsed selection, `deleteText(options?)` builds selected-text or caret-backspace/delete patches plus final collapsed selection, `rangeCount`/`selectedCount`/`hasSelection` expose cardinality, `isSelected(pointer)` is the per-item predicate, `togglePointer(pointer)` toggles one selected item including inside expanded ranges while preserving sparse selection, `selectedSource` feeds omitted `copy`/`cut`/`remove` sources and empty selection returns `empty_selection`, and `primaryPointer` feeds omitted `move`/`duplicate` sources plus `replace`/`paste` targets |
| `doc.clipboard` / `createClipboard(args)` | headless JSON fragment buffer; `copy`/`cut` accept `Pointer | Pointer[]` or default to current selection, `write` normalizes source metadata, multi-source payloads are JSON arrays, `read()` preserves `source`/`sources`, and `paste` accepts a Pointer or defaults to the primary selection target while spreading multi-source buffers into array targets by default unless `{ spread: false }` |

React is a facade adapter, not the owner of the editing model. If a workflow can
be expressed through `useJSONDocument`, it should also be expressible through
`createJSONDocument` in core/headless code.
Use `createSelection(ops)` for standalone headless selection/caret state.
Use `doc.commit(patch, { selection, label, origin, mergeKey })` when an editor
engine already knows the final caret/range after a patch. Empty patch +
selection updates selection without document patches or undo entries.
Selection actions and snapshots may carry `context`; it is recorded with
`selectionAfter` and restored by undo/redo.
Use `moveSelectionCursor`, `extendSelectionCursor`, and
`resolveSelectionCursor` for pure headless source-order cursor movement over a
`SelectionSnap` plus current JSON state, pass `query` for RFC 9535 JSONPath
result order, or pass explicit `points` for filtered, folded, virtualized, or
otherwise app-visible order.
Use `selectSelectionScope` and `resolveSelectionScope` for pure headless
select-all/select-visible/select-find-results behavior over the same traversal
options.
Use `orderSelectionRange`, `orderPrimarySelectionRange`, and
`orderSelectionRanges` when a command needs document-order `start`/`end` bounds
from directional anchor/focus selection.
Use `selectionSpansForPointer` or `doc.selection.spansForPointer(pointer)` when
rendering or editing needs pointer-local selected spans; pass `getLength` for
non-string offset domains.
Use `selectionTextEdits` / `doc.selection.textEdits(replacement)` to turn
selection into ordered pointer-local edit plans. Use `replaceSelectionText` /
`doc.selection.textPatch(replacement)` and `deleteSelectionText` /
`doc.selection.deleteText(options?)` for JSON string leaves; they return patch
plus final selection for `doc.commit`. Use `doc.commands.replaceText(...)` or
`doc.commands.deleteText(...)` when the string-leaf edit should commit through
document history directly; options may include `label`, `origin`, and
`mergeKey` history metadata.
Use `doc.ops` for low-level JSON operations through the document facade; the
standalone `JSONOps<T>` type is the shared ops contract for headless
composition.
Use `createClipboard(args)` for standalone headless clipboard state.
Use `doc.history` and `doc.commands.undo()` / `doc.commands.redo()` for
headless undo/redo state.
`doc.commands.select(action)`, `doc.commands.moveCursor(direction, options?)`,
`doc.commands.extendCursor(direction, options?)`, and
`doc.commands.selectScope(options?)` default to the document's configured
selection mode, so headless and React facades preserve the same multi-select
behavior and the full `SelectionSnap`, including `context`.
`doc.commands.replaceText(replacement, options?)` and
`doc.commands.deleteText(options?)` use that same `SelectionSnap` to commit JSON
string-leaf text edits with optional `label`, `origin`, and `mergeKey`
metadata; `doc.check.replaceText` / `doc.can.replaceText` and
`doc.check.deleteText` / `doc.can.deleteText` dry-run the same plan.

## 10 Verbs

| Pillar | Verbs |
| --- | --- |
| Selection | `select`, `find` |
| Edit | `move`, `duplicate`, `replace` |
| Clipboard | `cut`, `copy`, `paste` |
| Undo | `undo`, `redo` |

`JSONOps` stays close to RFC 6902: `add`, `remove`, `replace`, `move`, `copy`,
`test`, `patch`, `apply`, plus lifecycle helpers. New convenience verbs should
not be added unless they change the 4-pillar closure.

The schema gate dry-applies the patch and runs whole-document
`schema.safeParse`, so cross-field `.refine` / `.superRefine` violations are
rejected before commit.

JSONPath follows RFC 9535 selectors and filter function extensions:
`length`, `count`, `match`, `search`, and `value`.
The vendored RFC 9535 CTS gates full 703/703 conformance.

## Canonical Patterns

### React facade

```tsx
const doc = useJSONDocument(Schema, initialState, {
  history: 100,
  selection: { mode: "multiple" },
});

doc.ops.replace("/title", "final");
doc.commands.undo();
```

### Headless facade

```ts
const doc = createJSONDocument(Schema, initialState, { history: 100 });

doc.ops.add("/items/-", item);
doc.commit(
  [{ op: "replace", path: "/blocks", value: nextBlocks }],
  {
    label: "insertText",
    origin: "editor",
    selection: { type: "collapse", point: { path: "/blocks/0", offset: 2 } },
  },
);
doc.history.transaction(() => {
  doc.ops.replace("/title", "final");
  doc.ops.add("/logs/-", "renamed");
});
```

### Pure patch

```ts
const ops = [{ op: "replace", path: "/title", value: "final" }];
const inverse = computeInverses(prev, ops);
const applied = applyPatch(Schema, prev, ops);

if (applied.result.ok && inverse.ok) {
  commit(applied.state);
}
```

### Dict-record one key

For `z.record(z.string(), V)`, edit the exact key path. Do not replace the
whole record with a spread.

```ts
const writeKey = (k: string, v: V | undefined) => {
  const path = `/cells/${k}` as const;
  if (v === undefined && cells[k] !== undefined) doc.ops.remove(path);
  else if (v !== undefined && cells[k] === undefined) doc.ops.add(path, v);
  else if (v !== undefined && cells[k] !== v) doc.ops.replace(path, v);
};
```

### Clipboard

Clipboard verbs are headless JSON fragment operations. They do not call
`navigator.clipboard`.

```ts
const copied = doc.commands.copy("/items/0");
if (copied.ok) {
  doc.commands.paste(copied.payload, "/items/-");
}

doc.commands.select({ type: "addRange", pointer: "/items/0" });
const selected = doc.commands.copy();
if (selected.ok) doc.commands.paste(selected.payload, "after");

doc.commands.select({ type: "addRange", pointer: "/items/1" });
const multi = doc.commands.copy();
if (multi.ok) doc.commands.paste(multi.payload, "after", { spread: true });
```

### Check

Use `doc.check` when UI or agent code needs the same answer as `doc.can` plus
the failure code and reason. It does not mutate value, selection, clipboard, or
history.

```ts
const result = doc.check.replace("/items/0/name", value);
if (!result.ok && result.code === "schema_violation") show(result.violations);

const changed = doc.commands.replace("$.items[*].done", true);
const replaceable = doc.can.replace("$.items[*].done", true);
const findable = doc.check.find("$.items[*].name");
const cursor = doc.check.moveCursor("next", { points: visiblePoints });
const all = doc.check.selectScope({ points: visiblePoints });
```

`find` syntax failures report `syntax_error`; JSONPath replace with no matches
reports `empty_match`; `moveCursor` / `extendCursor` failures report
`cursor_boundary`; `selectScope` with no selectable points reports
`empty_scope`.

### Read / Query

Use facade reads instead of reimplementing Pointer or JSONPath traversal in UI
code. `doc.query` returns pointers, not values.

```ts
const title = doc.at("/title");
const itemPointers = doc.query("$.items[*].id");
const children = doc.entries("/items");
```

### Schema

Use `doc.schema` to ask what a path can contain without reaching into Zod
internals. Results are serializable and advisory; commits still go through the
schema gate.

```ts
const kind = doc.schema.kind("/items", "insert");
const accepts = doc.schema.accepts("/items", item, "insert");
```

### Burst edits

Use local UI preview for drag/IME. Use
`doc.commit(patch, { selection, label, origin, mergeKey })` when a patch and
final model selection should be one undo entry. For synchronous multi-step
document edits, use `doc.history.transaction({ label, origin, mergeKey }, fn)`. For
already-committed bursts, use `doc.history.mergeLast({ mergeKey })`. History
metadata is serializable.

## Source Of Truth

- Code behavior is the first source of truth.
- If code conflicts with RFC 6901/6902/9535/8259 or relevant wire standards,
  the RFC wins and code plus docs must be fixed.
- `packages/zod-crud/SPEC.md` describes current behavior; update it with
  behavior changes.
- Public export files: `packages/zod-crud/src/index.ts` and
  `packages/zod-crud/src/react.ts`.
- Site docs support `::source{path="..." lines="..."}`; source references are
  resolved during `npm run build -w @zod-crud/site`.

## Verification

```sh
npm run typecheck
npm test
npm run build
npm run smoke:package
npm run verify
```

Package publish guard: `npm run verify` through `prepublishOnly`.
